dialogue: A survivor camp has gathered around a young woman struggling to breathe.
Each breath sounds painful.
'She's out of medication,' someone says.
'The pharmacy was picked clean weeks ago.'

choices.text: Ask where they last found medicine

choices.lockedText: You don't know enough to help.

dialogue: You aren't a doctor, but the situation is obvious.
Without medication, she may not survive much longer.

choices.text: Ask where medicine might still be found

dialogue: 'There was an inhaler at a nearby house,' a survivor says.
'At least that's what we heard.'
'Problem is another family lives there now.'

choices.lockedText: You don't know where else to begin.

dialogue: A family answers through a chained door.
They're frightened but armed.
When asked about medicine, their expressions change.

choices.lockedText: You don't have enough to bargain with.

dialogue: 'We know exactly who you're talking about,' the father says.
'But my son needs that inhaler too.'
A teenager coughs from inside the house.
After a long discussion, the family considers it.
'Maybe we can work something out.'
You spend hours checking nearby homes.
Most are empty.
One medicine cabinet contains an expired inhaler.
Neither side is happy.
Neither side gets everything they want.
But both survivors have a chance now.
The camp accepts the inhaler.
Whether it works well enough is another question.
At least you tried.
The door slams shut.
Several weapons appear behind nearby windows.
The conversation is over.
You return with nothing.
The camp understands.
That doesn't make it easier.
There are too many people in need these days.
You continue down the road.
